item.title: [MIRROR-227] Implement Scheduling UI for Connected Professionals
item.description: Design and implement the Scheduling UI that allows users to view, book, and manage appointments with connected professionals. This screen should integrate seamlessly with the existing list of connected professionals and follow the app’s current design system and navigation flow.
item.summary: Implement Scheduling UI for Connected Professionals

item.title: [MIRROR-72] Refactor services to use new API service
item.description: We need to refactor existing services to integrate with the new ApiService implementation. However, progress is currently blocked because the backend returns nested JSON structures that do not match the expected format.
item.summary: Refactor services to use new API service

comments.comment: Still receiving nested Jsons, Will make a new Ticket to migrate in Refactor Branch and work with Shreyas to resolve the issue.

item.title: [MIRROR-36] Refactor Journal Module
item.description:
Refactor the existing Journal Module to improve maintainability, scalability, and readability. This involves optimizing widgets, implementing reusable components, leveraging mixins/extensions, integrating the base layer, refactoring BLoC logic, and removing unnecessary boilerplate code.

  • Acceptance Criteria:
    • Journal module uses reusable and optimized widgets.
    • Mixins & extensions implemented where applicable.
    • Base layer integrated cleanly.
    • BLoC logic simplified and optimized.
    • Boilerplate code removed.
    • No regression in existing Journal functionality.
item.summary: Refactor Journal Module

The goal is to have a draft version ready for review before merging into the main branch.

item.title: [MIRROR-180] Refactor & Integrate Profile Screen with BaseBloc, Add Reusable Widgets, and Separate Page/Screen
item.description:
Summary

We need to implement the Profile screen using clean architecture principles. This includes integrating with BaseBloc for state management and refactoring the screen into smaller reusable widgets.

Context

The Profile screen should adhere to the project's conventions. It needs to be split into two components: ProfilePage and ProfileScreen.

Acceptance criteria

  • Integrate the Profile screen with BaseBloc for state management.
  • Refactor the Profile screen into smaller reusable widgets.
  • Create ProfilePage for route-level setup and arguments handling.
  • Create ProfileScreen for UI layout and child widgets.
item.summary: Refactor & Integrate Profile Screen with BaseBloc, Add Reusable Widgets, and Separate Page/Screen

item.title: [MIRROR-141] Refactor Feedback Bloc & Integrate it with BaseBloc
item.summary: Refactor Feedback Bloc & Integrate it with BaseBloc

item.summary: Remove Unnecessary Boilerplate Code from Journal Module

item.summary: Refactor and Simplify Journal Module BLoC Logic

item.summary: Implement Reusable Widgets

item.title: [MIRROR-220] Implement Emergency Contact Feature MirrorConnect
item.summary: Implement Emergency Contact Feature MirrorConnect

item.title: [MIRROR-200] Insight not AutoScrolling on New Generated Messages
item.summary: Insight not AutoScrolling on New Generated Messages

item.title: [MIRROR-197] Remove redundant textScaler from MarkdownTextBuilder
item.description:
Remove the redundant textScaler: TextScaler.linear(1.0) property from the MarkdownStyleSheet configuration in MarkdownTextBuilder since explicit heading styles (h1-h6) are now defined.

Background:

The MarkdownTextBuilder component previously used textScaler: TextScaler.linear(1.0) to control text scaling, but this is no longer necessary since we now have explicit style definitions for all heading levels (h1-h6) with proper font weights and sizing.

Technical Details:

  • The textScaler property was used to prevent automatic text scaling
  • Now that we have explicit heading styles defined, the textScaler is redundant
  • Removing it will simplify the code and rely on the defined heading styles
item.summary: Remove redundant textScaler from MarkdownTextBuilder

item.title: [MIRROR-199] Template Pad Discard Bug
item.description: When I write a Template and don't save it, and discard it, and do back manually, it keeps me in a loop around template pad and template screen.
item.summary: Template Pad Discard Bug

item.title: [MIRROR-195] Fix MarkdownList Spacing in Chat Messages
item.description:
Implemented conditional block spacing for markdown rendering in chat messages to improve the visual appearance of lists while maintaining proper paragraph spacing.

Changes Made:

  1. MarkdownTextBuilder Component Enhancement
  • Added conditional block spacing logic using hasLists() function
  • Reduced spacing from 24px to 12px when lists are detected in content
  • Maintained 24px spacing for regular paragraph content
  • Added import for common_utils.dart to support list detection functionality
  1. Chat Message Bubble
  • Updated to use the enhanced MarkdownTextBuilder with improved spacing
  1. Configuration Updates
  • Modified app_config.dart (specific changes need review)
  • Updated pubspec.yaml dependencies
  • Android build configuration updates in build.gradle

Technical Details:

  • Uses regex pattern matching to detect bullet points (-, *, +) and numbered lists
  • Conditional spacing: 12px for content with lists, 24px for regular paragraphs
  • Maintains backward compatibility with existing markdown rendering
item.summary: Fix MarkdownList Spacing in Chat Messages

item.title: [MIRROR-192] Implement Markdown Rendering Support for Chat Messages
item.description:
This ticket covers the implementation of markdown text rendering capabilities for chat messages in the Mirror Connect Flutter application. The changes enable rich text formatting in chat conversations, improving user experience by supporting formatted content display.

Changes Implemented

1. New Component: MarkdownTextBuilder

File: app/lib/presentation/components/markdown_text_builder.dart

  • ✅ Created a new reusable widget for rendering markdown content
  • ✅ Features implemented:
  • Supports customizable text color and text style
  • Uses markdown_widget package for markdown parsing and rendering
  • Configurable paragraph styling with 1.6 line height
  • Zero margins for tight layout control
  • Responsive to theme colors (supports both light/dark themes)

Key Properties:

  • content: Required string containing markdown content
  • textColor: Optional color override for text
  • textStyle: Optional text style override

2. Updated Component: ChatMessageBubble

File: app/lib/presentation/components/chat_message_bubble.dart

  • ✅ Integrated MarkdownTextBuilder into chat message rendering
  • ✅ Enhanced message display:
  • Replaced plain text rendering with markdown support
  • Maintains existing theming and color schemes
  • Preserves responsive layout and bubble styling
  • Supports different text colors for sent vs received messages

Integration Details:

  • Uses MarkdownTextBuilder in _buildMessageText() method
  • Passes appropriate text colors based on message type (sent/received)
  • Maintains existing Flexible widget wrapper for proper layout

3. Dependency Addition: pubspec.yaml

File: app/pubspec.yaml

  • ✅ Added markdown_widget: 2.3.2+8 dependency
  • ✅ Positioned correctly in the "Widgets" section alongside other UI dependencies
item.summary: Implement Markdown Rendering Support for Chat Messages

item.title: [MIRROR-135] Save button disabled after pasting into Title, Content on Template Pad
item.description: When pasting text into the Template field, the Save button remains greyed out (disabled) even though the field contains valid input.
item.summary: Save button disabled after pasting into Title, Content on Template Pad

item.title: [MIRROR-115] Profile Screen is not being refreshed on updating the data fields
item.summary: Profile Screen is not being refreshed on updating the data fields

item.title: [MIRROR-82] Add Abstract Layer User Remote Data Source & update bloc, usecases
item.summary: Add Abstract Layer User Remote Data Source & update bloc, usecases

item.title: [MIRROR-109] Refactor Data Flow: Use Either<Failure, Data> Across Local Data Sources, UseCases, and BLoC
item.description:
Summary

Currently, local data source functions directly return raw data types or throw exceptions. This causes inconsistent error handling across layers.
We need to migrate all Local Data Sources, UseCases, and BLoC to follow the functional error handling pattern using Either<Failure, Data>.

Scope

  • Update all local data source methods to return Either<Failure, Data> instead of raw types or thrown errors.
  • Refactor affected UseCases to handle Either values correctly and propagate them upwards.
  • Update BLoC states/events to properly process Left (failure) and Right (success).
  • Ensure error messages, loading, and success flows are aligned across the presentation layer.

Acceptance Criteria

  • All local data source methods return Either<Failure, Data>.
  • UseCases map Left to domain Failure objects and Right to valid entities.
  • BLoC handles failures via Left and updates state accordingly.
  • Unit tests updated to cover both success (Right) and failure (Left) cases.
  • No direct exceptions thrown from data sources → handled via Either.
item.summary: Refactor Data Flow: Use Either<Failure, Data> Across Local Data Sources, UseCases, and BLoC

item.title: [MIRROR-116] Feedback selection is not closing after selection
item.description:
The feedback selection under feedback screen , after selecting the value it's getting selected but the popup is not getting closed.

UX Issue : After reporting anything we should show some user friendly message ie: "Thank you for submitting your

{variable type}

! We truly value your input and will review your request soon. Your feedback helps us make the app even better for you!" Instead or navigating back so fast  that user even don’t understand what just happened.
item.summary: Feedback selection is not closing after selection

item.title: [MIRROR-121] UI : Alert banner content is not visible.
item.description: Anurag will provide updated colours and UI presentation.
item.summary: UI : Alert banner content is not visible.

item.title: [MIRROR-91] Fix Padding for Banner on Journey Screen
item.description:
Impact

The bug causes incorrect padding on the banner when the display is scaled. This leads to a distorted appearance of the banner, affecting user experience.

Expected behaviour

The banner should maintain consistent padding regardless of display scaling. It should appear properly aligned and visually appealing.

Actual behaviour

Currently, the banner has weird padding when the display is scaled. This results in a misaligned and unprofessional look.

Steps to reproduce

  • Scale the display to different sizes.
  • Observe the padding on the banner.
item.summary: Fix Padding for Banner on Journey Screen

item.title: [MIRROR-92] Fix: Journey Screen not refreshing after returning from Insight Screen
item.description: When navigating from the Journey Screen to the Insight Screen and making changes (e.g., adding or updating journal entries), the Journey Screen does not refresh automatically upon returning. This causes outdated data to be shown until the user manually refreshes or reopens the app.
item.summary: Fix: Journey Screen not refreshing after returning from Insight Screen

item.title: [MIRROR-76] Refactor Remote Data Sources to Abstract Layer
item.summary: Refactor Remote Data Sources to Abstract Layer

item.title: [MIRROR-84] Integrate Connect Professional Analytics API
item.description:
Description

Integrate analytics API to track user interactions when connecting with professionals in the Connect feature.

Acceptance Criteria

  • [ ] Analytics API endpoint integrated for professional connections
  • [ ] User and professional information captured in analytics
  • [ ] Professional connection requests tracked with proper metadata
  • [ ] Error handling implemented for analytics API calls
  • [ ] No impact on existing user experience

Technical Implementation

  • Added updateProfessionalAnalytics method to professional repository layer
  • Enhanced UserAnalyticsService to capture user and professional names
  • Updated SendConnectRequestUsecase to include professional name parameter
  • Added getCurrentUserDisplayName() method to AuthService
  • Integrated analytics tracking in connect request flow

Analytics Data Captured

  • Screen name: /connect
  • Action: view
  • User ID and display name
  • Professional ID and name
  • Timestamp information

Files Modified

  • dependency_injector.dart
  • auth_service.dart
  • user_analytics_service.dart
  • professional_remote_data_source.dart
  • professional_repository_impl.dart
  • professional_repository.dart
  • send_connect_request_usecase.dart
  • connect_bloc.dart

Definition of Done

  • [ ] Analytics API integration complete
  • [ ] All unit tests passing
  • [ ] Code review approved
  • [ ] No breaking changes to existing functionality
item.summary: Integrate Connect Professional Analytics API

item.title: [MIRROR-89] Fix Journey Screen Gray Out after Notification Permission
item.summary: Fix Journey Screen Gray Out after Notification Permission

item.title: [MIRROR-90] Fix Unlock Button Height
item.summary: Fix Unlock Button Height

item.title: [MIRROR-86] Enhance Alert Banner & Fix Popup Bug
item.description:
Summary

The issue involves changing the color of the alert banner to yellow. Additionally, there is a bug with the popup that needs to be fixed. The popup does not disappear after an item is deleted.

Context

The alert banner currently has a different color that needs to be changed. There is also a functional issue with the popup that affects user experience.

Acceptance criteria

  • Change the color of the alert banner to yellow.
  • Fix the popup bug so that it disappears after deleting an item.

Other information

N/A
item.summary: Enhance Alert Banner & Fix Popup Bug

item.title: [MIRROR-83] Add time-based wellness banners to Journal Screen
item.description:
Summary

Add time-based wellness banners to the journal for Mirror users. These banners will change based on the time of day to provide relevant mindfulness prompts.

Context

The goal is to enhance the user experience by displaying contextual wellness prompts. These prompts will adapt to the time of day, encouraging mindfulness.

Acceptance criteria

  • Banner displays at the top of the journal list.
  • Morning theme (6am-6pm): Plant SVG with "Find your calm" message.
  • Evening theme (6pm-6am): Mindful SVG with "Prioritize your well-being" message.
  • Text content uses localization strings.
  • Responsive layout adapts to different screen sizes.
  • Banner integrates seamlessly with the existing journal list.

Other information

Technical Notes:

  • Create TimeBasedBanner component.
  • Use getCurrentLocalTimeWithTimezone() for time detection.
  • Implement with MediaQuery for responsive design.
  • Add localization keys: journalMorningTitle, journalEveningTitle, etc.

Design Assets:

  • banner_plant.svg
  • banner_mindful.svg
item.summary: Add time-based wellness banners to Journal Screen

item.title: [MIRROR-69] Breathing indicator color sync & stream rate optimization
item.description:
• Update the breathing indicator to match the persona color currently in use.
  • Add control over the stream rate to smoothen the experience.
  • Allow room for experimentation with the implementation to find the best balance between performance and smoothness.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Breathing indicator dynamically reflects persona color.
  • Stream rate adjustments result in smoother user experience.
  • No regression in current breathing animation or streaming functionality.
item.summary: Breathing indicator color sync & stream rate optimization

item.title: [MIRROR-75] Implement Abstract Layer Pattern Across Core Services
item.description:
Summary

Refactor multiple core services to implement abstract layer patterns. This will enhance code maintainability, testability, and adherence to clean architecture principles. Interfaces will be introduced for all major services, separating implementation from contracts.

Context

The goal is to improve the architecture of core services by using abstract layer patterns. This change aims to make the codebase cleaner and more manageable.

Acceptance criteria

  • Extract abstract interfaces for all core services.
  • Refactor concrete implementations to extend abstract interfaces.
  • Maintain existing functionality while improving architecture.
  • Ensure consistent naming conventions (Service + ServiceImpl).
  • Verify all public methods are properly abstracted.
  • Update service implementations to follow interface contracts.
item.summary: Implement Abstract Layer Pattern Across Core Services

item.title: [MIRROR-74] Add Support for Google Play's 16 KB page size
item.summary: Add Support for Google Play's 16 KB page size

item.title: [MIRROR-62] Initial API Service Architecture Migrations
item.description:
Summary

Migrate the existing HTTP-based API service to use Dio implementation. This will include improved error handling, retry mechanisms, streaming capabilities, and robust response management. The goal is to replace the current HTTP client while ensuring backward compatibility and enhancing reliability and performance.

Context

The current API service is based on HTTP. The migration aims to implement Dio for better functionality and performance. Key components include replacing the HTTP client, adding retry logic, and creating an abstraction layer.

Acceptance criteria

  • [ ] Replace HTTP client with Dio implementation in ApiServiceImpl
  • [ ] Implement custom Dio interceptors (CustomInterceptor) for authentication and logging
  • [ ] Add retry mechanism with exponential backoff (3 retries with 1s, 2s, 3s delays)
  • [ ] Implement IResponse interface with SuccessResponse and ErrorResponse classes
  • [ ] Support all HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE, multipart) with proper error handling
  • [ ] Update UserRemoteDataSource to use new API service with Either<NwFailure, Option<None>> pattern
  • [ ] Update dependency injection in dependency_injector.dart to use new ApiServiceImpl
  • [ ] Maintain backward compatibility with existing service interfaces

Other information

  • Refactor & Documentation Added
item.summary: Initial API Service Architecture Migrations

item.title: [MIRROR-33] Research Retrofit and Chopper packages for API integration
item.description:
I need to evaluate the Retrofit and Chopper packages in Flutter for API integration to determine which is more suitable for our project.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Document how Retrofit works with examples (setup, API definition, request handling, error handling).
  • Document how Chopper works with examples (setup, API definition, request handling, error handling).
  • Compare both packages in terms of:
    • Ease of integration
    • Interceptor support (auth, retry, logging)
    • Code generation (pros/cons)
    • Community support & maintenance
    • Performance and scalability
  • Provide a short recommendation on which package better suits our project needs.
item.summary: Research Retrofit and Chopper packages for API integration
